Narsipatnam, Anakapalli District,
Andhra Pradesh - 531116.

Avanthi Institute of Engineering & Technology was established in **1991** by Sri. Muttamsetti Srinivasa Rao (Avanthi Srinivas), under the Avanthi Educational Society. It is located at Narsipatnam, Anakapalli District, Andhra Pradesh - 531116. The college offers B.Tech, M.Tech, and Diploma programs across multiple engineering branches.
No worries at all! I'm here whenever you need me 😊

Admissions open every year in June. You need to fill out the online application form on our website, submit your 12th grade marksheet, and clear the entrance exam. The cutoff is typically 60% aggregate.
To apply, visit our official website, complete the application form, and upload required documents including your academic transcripts and ID proof.
We offer undergraduate programs in Engineering (CS, IT, ECE, Mechanical, Civil), Science (Physics, Chemistry, Math), Commerce, and Arts. Postgraduate programs include MBA, MCA, M.Tech, and M.Sc.
Our college offers B.Tech, B.Sc, B.Com, BA, MBA, MCA, M.Tech, and Ph.D programs across various disciplines.

**Fee Structure:**

| Program | Annual Fee |
|---|---|
| Engineering (B.Tech) | ₹1,00,000 |
| Science (B.Sc) | ₹60,000 |
| Commerce / Arts | ₹50,000 |

Fee range: ₹49,500 – ₹1,20,000 depending on program. Scholarships available for meritorious students. Pay via the **Parent Portal App**. No credentials? Contact your class incharge or HOD.

patterns: where can i pay my fees
responses:
You can pay your fees through the **Parent Portal App** — just log in with your credentials and complete the payment online. Easy and quick! 💳

If you don't have your login credentials, just contact your class incharge or HOD and they'll help you get access.

Fee payment is done through the **Parent Portal App**. Log in, go to the payments section, and you're good to go! If you're having trouble with credentials, reach out to your class incharge or HOD — they'll sort it out.

patterns: what if i dont have credentials
what should i do if i dont have credentials
responses: If you don't have your Parent Portal credentials, please contact or meet your class incharge or HOD directly. They will help you get your login details so you can access the portal and pay your fees.
Semester exams are held in November-December (odd semester) and April-May (even semester). Internal assessments are conducted monthly. Check the college website or notice board for exact dates.
Exam schedules are published on the college portal 30 days before exams. Internal exams happen every month and carry 30% weightage.
Results are published on the college student portal within 30 days of exam completion. Log in with your student ID to view your grades and download your marksheet.
You can check your results on the official college portal under the 'Student Results' section. Results are typically declared within 4 weeks after exams.
Yes, we have separate hostels for boys and girls with 24/7 security, Wi-Fi, mess facility, and laundry services. Hostel fees are ₹80,000/year including meals. Apply during admission.
On-campus accommodation is available. The hostel has AC and non-AC rooms. Facilities include gym, common room, and high-speed internet.
The college library is open from 8 AM to 8 PM on weekdays and 9 AM to 5 PM on weekends. It has over 50,000 books, e-journals, and digital resources. Students can borrow up to 3 books for 14 days.
Our library offers physical books, online journals (IEEE, Springer), and a reading room. Library cards are issued during orientation week.

**Placement Highlights:**

- Placement rate: 85%+
- Average package: ₹5.5 LPA
- Highest package: ₹24 LPA

**Top Recruiters:**
- TCS, Infosys, Wipro
- Accenture, Amazon
- 150+ companies visit annually

Placement season: October – February. The placement cell also assists with internships, resume building, and mock interviews.

We have excellent sports facilities including a cricket ground, football field, basketball and volleyball courts, a fully equipped gym, and an indoor badminton court. Annual sports day is held in January.
Sports facilities are available for all students. We participate in inter-college tournaments. The college has produced state-level athletes in cricket, athletics, and chess.
You can reach us at: Phone: +91-XXXXXXXXXX, Email: info@college.edu, Address: 123 College Road, City - 000000. Office hours are 9 AM to 5 PM, Monday to Saturday.
For queries, contact the admin office at admin@college.edu or call +91-XXXXXXXXXX. The principal's office can be reached at principal@college.edu.
College hours are 8:30 AM to 4:30 PM, Monday to Saturday. Classes begin at 9:00 AM. The college is closed on Sundays and public holidays.
Regular classes run from 9 AM to 4 PM. The college office opens at 8:30 AM. Saturday has classes till 1 PM.

We have 20+ student clubs including Coding Club, Robotics Club, Drama Club, Music Club, NSS, NCC, and Literary Club. Annual cultural fest 'Utsav' is held in February with competitions and performances.
Student activities include technical fests, cultural programs, hackathons, and community service. Joining clubs helps build skills and network beyond academics.

patterns: How to reach college by bus
responses: College buses operate on 15+ routes covering major areas of the city. Transport fee varies by distance. Bus passes are issued at the start of each semester. Contact the transport office for route details.

patterns: is the gym available for hostelers
responses: Timings for the Gym: 6 AM to 9 AM, Evening 3 PM to 9 PM. Location: Diploma block backside.

patterns: what labs does the college have
responses:
Here are all the labs in our college:

1. 🖥️ JKC Lab (Computer) — CSE dept, 3rd floor, Main Block
2. 🖥️ Ground Floor Lab (Computer) — Main Block
3. 🖥️ New Lab-1 & Lab-2 (Computer) — New Building, Ground Floor
4. 📡 ECE Lab (Electronics) — 2nd floor, Main Block
5. ⚡ EEE Lab (Electrical) — 2nd floor, Main Block
6. ⚙️ Mech Lab (Mechanical/Workshop) — Near Cricket Court
7. 🧪 Chemistry Lab — Science Block, Ground Floor
8. 🔬 Physics Lab — Science Block, First Floor
9. 📐 CEW Lab — 3rd floor, Decennial Block

Ask me about any specific lab for more details!

Chemistry Lab is located in the Science Block, Ground Floor. It is equipped with fume hoods, chemical storage cabinets, distillation setups, titration equipment, and safety gear. Lab timings: 9 AM to 4 PM on working days. Capacity: 30 students per batch.

Physics Lab is located in the Science Block, First Floor. It has equipment for optics, mechanics, electricity and magnetism experiments including spectrometers, sonometers, potentiometers, and CRO. Lab timings: 9 AM to 4 PM on working days. Capacity: 30 students per batch.

Computer Labs are in the Main Block. JKC Lab is on the 3rd floor (CSE dept), Ground Floor Lab is in the main block, and New Lab-1 & Lab-2 are in the new building (ground floor). All labs have high-speed internet, 200+ systems with Windows/Linux, and software like VS Code, Python, Java, MATLAB. Timings: 9 AM to 5 PM.

Mechanical Lab is located near the Cricket Court. It includes Workshop Lab, Fluid Mechanics Lab, Thermodynamics Lab, and CAD/CAM Lab. Equipment includes lathes, milling machines, drilling machines, heat exchangers, and CNC machines. Lab timings: 9 AM to 4 PM. Safety gear is mandatory.

EEE Lab is on the 2nd floor of the Main Block. It has Electrical Machines Lab, Power Electronics Lab, Control Systems Lab, and Switchgear Lab. Equipment includes DC/AC machines, transformers, power converters, and PLC trainers. Lab timings: 9 AM to 4 PM.

ECE Lab is on the 2nd floor of the Main Block. It includes Digital Electronics Lab, Analog Communication Lab, VLSI Lab, Embedded Systems Lab, and Microprocessor Lab. Equipment includes DSOs, function generators, FPGA boards, Arduino/Raspberry Pi kits, and spectrum analyzers. Lab timings: 9 AM to 4 PM.
VLSI Lab is located in the ECE Lab on the 2nd floor of the Main Block. It has FPGA boards, VLSI design tools, and simulation software. Lab timings: 9 AM to 4 PM.
Digital Electronics Lab is part of the ECE Lab on the 2nd floor of the Main Block. It has logic trainers, digital ICs, and oscilloscopes. Lab timings: 9 AM to 4 PM.
Embedded Systems Lab is part of the ECE Lab on the 2nd floor of the Main Block. It has Arduino kits, Raspberry Pi boards, microcontrollers, and IoT development tools. Lab timings: 9 AM to 4 PM.
Microprocessor Lab is part of the ECE Lab on the 2nd floor of the Main Block. It has 8086/8051 microprocessor kits, interfacing boards, and assembly language tools. Lab timings: 9 AM to 4 PM.
Power Systems Lab is part of the EEE Lab on the 2nd floor of the Main Block. It has DC/AC machines, transformers, power converters, and power electronics trainers. Lab timings: 9 AM to 4 PM.
Control Systems Lab is part of the EEE Lab on the 2nd floor of the Main Block. It has PLC trainers, control system simulators, and feedback control kits. Lab timings: 9 AM to 4 PM.

Workshop Lab is part of the Mechanical Lab located near the Cricket Court. It has lathe machines, milling machines, drilling machines, and CNC machines. Lab timings: 9 AM to 4 PM. Safety gear is mandatory.
Fluid Mechanics Lab is part of the Mechanical Lab near the Cricket Court. It has flow measurement equipment, pumps, turbines, and hydraulic benches. Lab timings: 9 AM to 4 PM.

JKC Lab (Computer Lab) is on the 3rd floor of the Main Block, managed by the CSE department. It has 200+ systems with Windows/Linux, high-speed internet, and software like VS Code, Python, Java, and MATLAB. Timings: 9 AM to 5 PM.
CEW Lab is on the 3rd floor of the Decennial Block. Lab timings: 9 AM to 4 PM.

patterns: which coding language should i learn
how does social media marketing work
responses:
That's a great topic! Here's a quick overview:

- **AI** — Simulation of human intelligence in machines; enables learning and decision-making.
- **Data Science** — Uses statistics, programming & ML to extract insights from data.
- **Cyber Security** — Protecting systems and data from digital attacks using encryption, firewalls etc.
- **Cloud Computing** — Accessing computing resources over the internet (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud).
- **Python** — Widely used for AI, data science, and web development due to its simplicity.

Want to learn any of these? Ask me about CRT training or online courses! 😊

patterns: where can i find free learning resources
can i learn new skills for free
responses: You can learn online for free on platforms like **Coursera**, **Udemy**, **Khan Academy**, **NPTEL**, and **YouTube**. Many offer certificates too! For college-specific training, check out our **CRT program** which covers aptitude, C, Python, and DSA. 🎓

patterns: how can i advance my career
how do i get a better job
what should i learn for job
responses:
**Tips to grow your career:**

- Build strong fundamentals in your core subject
- Learn in-demand skills: Python, Data Science, Cloud, Cyber Security
- Get certified (NPTEL, Coursera, AWS)
- Participate in our **CRT program** for placement prep
- Build projects and a strong resume
- Network and attend college placement drives

Our TPO Dr. T. Srinivas Rao can guide you further! 🚀

**CSE (Computer Science & Engineering):**

Duration: 4 years. Focuses on programming, data structures, algorithms, operating systems, databases, networking, AI, and cloud computing. Great for careers in software development, AI/ML, and IT. HOD: Mr. U Nanaji.
